dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 6717 IH: Military Financial Literacy Accountability Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Johnson of Texas (for herself and Mrs. Kiggans of Virginia) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Armed Services

form.official-title: To improve financial literacy training for members of the Armed Forces, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Military Financial Literacy Accountability Act.

section.header: Improvements to financial literacy training for members of the Armed Forces

subsection.header: Modifications to financial literacy and preparedness survey
subsection.text: Section 992(e) of title 10, United States Code, is amended—

subparagraph.text: by striking The Director of the Defense Manpower Data Center and inserting The Secretary of Defense; and
by striking armed forces. and inserting the following:

quoted-block.text: armed forces that includes prompts intended to identify the following:

subparagraph.text: The current level of financial literacy among members of the Armed Forces who are enlisted members in a pay grade of E–7 or below or commissioned officers in a pay grade of O–4 or below.
The preferred methods of financial education delivery for members of the Armed Forces, including whether members prefer—

clause.text: any other delivery methods identified by such members.

subparagraph.text: The specific financial topics of greatest interest and need to the members, including whether members are interested in—

clause.text: tuition assistance and education financing; or

subparagraph.text: Any barriers to participation in existing financial literacy training programs.
Recommendations from members of the Armed Forces for improving financial literacy training programs.
by redesignating subparagraphs (A) and (B) as subparagraphs (B) and (C), respectively; and
by inserting before subparagraph (B), as so redesignated, the following new subparagraph:
shall be compiled by the Director in such a way as to protect the privacy of the respondents to such survey;

paragraph.text: ensure the Secretaries concerned modify the appropriate administrative systems to increase the accuracy of tracking completion of the financial readiness common military training by members of the Armed Forces;
ensure the Secretaries concerned identify and address causes of non-completion of financial readiness common military training by members of the Armed Forces;

subparagraph.text: determining which standardized performance measures, if any, are needed to measure the effectiveness of the financial education efforts of the Department of Defense; and
the implementation of such performance measures; and

paragraph.text: submit to Congress an implementation timeline and strategy for carrying out the amendments made by subsection (a).
